{"step_by_step_thinking": "Transposition of great arteries with intact septum (A) does not require a patent ductus arteriosus for survival because the ventricles are correctly positioned, and the heart can function normally without the need for mixing of blood. Interrupted aortic arch (B), truncus arteriosus (C), and hypoplastic left heart syndrome (D) all require a patent ductus arteriosus to maintain blood flow and ensure survival.", "answer_choice": "A"}
